---
package: "openpipeline"
version: "v4.2.0"
title: "clear_slots"
namespace: "Transform"
description: "Clear one or more AnnData slot containers from a modality in an h5mu file"
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline v4.2.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `clear_slots`  
Namespace: `transform`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/tree/v4.2.0/src/transform/clear_slots)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/transform/clear_slots)

:::




## Argument group

### Arguments

|Name                   |Description                                                                                                    |Attributes                                      |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input`              |Input h5mu file.                                                                                               |`file`, required, example: `"input.h5mu"`       |
|`--modality`           |Which modality from the input MuData file to process.                                                          |`string`, default: `"rna"`                      |
|`--slots`              |Names of the AnnData slot families to clear from the selected modality.                                        |List of `string`, required, multiple_sep: `";"` |
|`--output`             |Output h5mu file.                                                                                              |`file`, required, default: `"output.h5mu"`      |
|`--output_compression` |Compression format to use for the output AnnData and/or Mudata H5 files. By default no compression is applied. |`string`, example: `"gzip"`                     |

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/transform/clear_slots/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/transform/clear_slots).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/transform/clear_slots/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).
